required_skills: Product Backlog Creation, Grooming, and Prioritization
User Story Writing with Detailed Acceptance Criteria
Definition of Done (DoD) and Definition of Ready (DoR) Governance
Cross-functional Team Coordination between Business and Engineering
Value-based Prioritization using Frameworks such as WSJF or MoSCoW
Product Vision Communication and Roadmap Alignment
preferred_skills: Certified Scrum Product Owner (CSPO) or SAFe POPM certification
Experience in scaled Agile frameworks such as SAFe or LeSS
Basic understanding of software architecture and APIs
Familiarity with UX design principles and usability heuristics
Knowledge of business process modeling and workflow automation
Quantitative analysis using product analytics tools
Experience working in regulated industries such as fintech or healthtech
Familiarity with Behavior-Driven Development (BDD) and Gherkin syntax
responsibilities: Own and manage the product backlog end-to-end, ensuring all items are clearly defined, estimated, and prioritized in alignment with business value and strategic goals
Write granular, testable user stories and acceptance criteria that give engineering teams unambiguous requirements and a clear definition of done
Lead sprint planning, backlog refinement, sprint review, and retrospective ceremonies in close partnership with the Scrum Master and development team
Serve as the primary liaison between business stakeholders and the engineering team, translating complex business needs into actionable technical requirements
Define and communicate the product vision and roadmap to the development team, ensuring every sprint delivers measurable progress toward product goals
Conduct acceptance testing on completed features, validating that deliverables meet acceptance criteria before sign-off and release to production
Prioritize backlog items using value-based frameworks such as WSJF or MoSCoW, balancing technical debt, new features, and bug fixes to maximize sprint output
Collaborate with UX designers to incorporate user research findings, usability feedback, and design prototypes into clearly scoped backlog items
Track and report sprint velocity, release burn-down, and backlog health metrics to senior stakeholders and delivery leadership on a regular cadence
Identify and proactively resolve blockers, scope creep, and dependency conflicts that threaten sprint commitments or release timelines
